problems.problem_description:
# Problem setup:
The Klyachko-Can-Binicioglu-Shumovsky (KCBS) scenario uses a single spin-1 (qutrit) system and five rank-1 yes/no measurements $\{\Pi_i=|v_i\rangle\langle v_i|\}_{i=0}^{4}$ arranged on a pentagram, where cyclically adjacent measurements are exclusive, $\langle v_i|v_{i+1}\rangle=0$ (indices mod 5). A noncontextual hidden-variable model obeys $\sum_{i=0}^{4}\langle\Pi_i\rangle\le 2$.

# Main problem:
Over all qutrit quantum states $\rho$, what is the maximum value of $\sum_{i=0}^{4}\mathrm{Tr}(\rho\,\Pi_i)$? Give the exact value.
problems.code_template:
def answer():
    r"""Return the maximum quantum value of the KCBS sum (float)."""
    # ------------------ FILL IN YOUR RESULTS BELOW ------------------

    # ---------------------------------------------------------------
    return kcbs_max
problems.answer_code:
def answer():
    r"""Return the maximum quantum value of the KCBS sum (float)."""
    # ------------------ FILL IN YOUR RESULTS BELOW ------------------
    kcbs_max = 5**0.5   # = 2.2360679...   # reference (verified)
    # ---------------------------------------------------------------
    return kcbs_max
